---
title: "Group analysis"
subtitle: "Fit feature-wise meta-regression from beta and variance matrices."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Inputs

Group analysis works on first-level estimates and their sampling variances.
Rows are subjects or studies; columns are voxels, parcels, or other features.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm

rng = np.random.default_rng(12)
n_subjects = 10
n_features = 6

group = np.repeat([0, 1], n_subjects // 2)
X = np.column_stack([np.ones(n_subjects), group])

true_effect = np.array([[0.2] * n_features, [0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.0, -0.1, -0.2]])
V = rng.uniform(0.04, 0.12, size=(n_subjects, n_features))
Y = X @ true_effect + rng.normal(scale=np.sqrt(V))

Y.shape, V.shape, X.shape
```

## Fit Meta-Regression

`fmri_meta_fit()` estimates one weighted model per feature. Use `"fe"` for a
fixed-effect fit and `"pm"` or `"dl"` for random-effects tau-squared estimates.

```{python}
fit = fm.fmri_meta_fit(Y, V, X, method="pm")

fit["beta"].shape, fit["se"].shape, fit["tau2"].round(4)
```

```{python}
summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": [f"feature_{i + 1}" for i in range(n_features)],
    "group_beta": fit["beta"][1],
    "group_se": fit["se"][1],
    "group_z": fit["z"][1],
    "p": fit["p"][1],
    "I2_fe": fit["I2_fe"],
})

summary.round(3)
```

## Covariance Output

When downstream code needs coefficient covariance matrices, use the covariance
variant.

```{python}
fit_cov = fm.fmri_meta_fit_cov(Y, V, X, method="pm")
fit_cov["cov_tri"].shape
```

## Next

- [Functional connectivity](functional-connectivity.qmd)
- [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd)
